Simulate a SpatialExperiment object with spaSim3D metadata.

Source: R/simulate_spe_metadata3D.R
simulate_spe_metadata3D.Rd

This function generates a SpatialExperiment object with parameters specified with inputted spaSim3D metadata. By doing so, it simulates a 3D tissue, beginning with the background cells of the tissue, then adding cell clusters to the tissue.

simulate_spe_metadata3D(
  spe_metadata,
  plot_image = TRUE,
  plot_cell_types = NULL,
  plot_colours = NULL
)

Arguments

spe_metadata

A list containing the metadata to generate the SpatialExperiment object. This is generated from the output of the spaSim3D functions 'spe_metadata_background_template' and 'spe_metadata_cluster_template'.

plot_image

A logical indicating whether to plot 3D spatial data with the added metadata. Defaults to TRUE.

plot_cell_types

A string vector specifying the cell types to plot. If NULL, all cell types in the feature_colname column will be considered. Defaults to NULL.

plot_colours

A string vector specifying the colours of the cell types when plotting. Must match the number of cell types specified in plot_cell_types. If NULL, the viridis color pallete will be used. Defaults to NULL.

Value

A SpatialExperiment object containing the cells of the simulated 3D tissue and including the inputted metadata.

Examples

# Get background metadata
bg_metadata <- spe_metadata_background_template("random")

# Get cluster metadata (using background metadata as background)
cluster_metadata <- spe_metadata_cluster_template("regular", "sphere", bg_metadata)
cluster_metadata <- spe_metadata_cluster_template("ring", "ellipsoid", cluster_metadata)

# Get spe from cluster metadata
spe_clusters <- simulate_spe_metadata3D(cluster_metadata)
#> Warning: plot_cell_types not specified, all cell types found in the spe object will be used.
#> Warning: plot_colours not specified, viridis (D) palette will be used.
# Re-plotting so it shows in GitHub pages
plot_cells3D(spe_clusters)
#> Warning: plot_cell_types not specified, all cell types found in the spe object will be used.
#> Warning: plot_colours not specified, viridis (D) palette will be used.
